title: Create shorter names for containers based on image IDs
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind bug

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

When we specify the image name to use for creating a container by using its ID, truncate the ID before appending the naming suffix.  An untruncated ID is already as long as the static HOST_NAME_MAX value used by some applications, and we started making the container's name the FQDN of the container in f8c152694c1edce4b9b1d80d926f94226caab90c, which would have created problems for those applications.

#### How to verify it

New integration test!

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

Fixes #3892.

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
Containers created by running "buildah from" and specifying the base image using its full ID, or during a "buildah build", will be given shorter auto-generated names.
```

comments.body: A clarifying question: the hostname here is composed of this 64-character alphanumeric prefix, plus a suffix, defaulting to `working-container` but configurable via `--suffix` -- is it still plausible that a subsequent change to the default suffix might raise these issues again? Is it worth further truncating the full name, say, [here](https://github.com/nalind/buildah-1/blob/a677a93059c1882bcde428a38600fd7d9e89b7eb/new.go#L224)?

My main aim with this PR is fixing the cases where we would create potential problems when the user doesn't specify anything, as happens in `buildah build`.  People who run `buildah from` have its `--name` option, so they have total control over the name.  While changing the default suffix by itself could reintroduce the problem, I'm not really worried about the default suffix changing in the future, so I'd be more concerned about a manually-specified name being longer than that.  Fetching the correct value would involve some cgo, though, and I'm on the fence about whether doing that is really worth it.
Thanks for fixing this issue so promptly and for the excellent follow-up comment!
